_comment: Tyk API definition for the AmabileAI Observability (Opik) stack. POST this body to https://tyk-gateway-production-2e1b.up.railway.app/tyk/apis with header 'x-tyk-authorization: $TYK_GW_SECRET', then hot-reload via GET/POST /tyk/reload/group. Mirrors the bitrix24-indufix conventions (listen_path with trailing slash + strip, preserve_host:false so the upstream sees its own Host, global rate limit + quota). DESIGN: keyless at the gateway (use_keyless:true) — Opik already enforces per-request identity via its own SDK API key on /api/v1/private/*, so a Tyk auth_token here would create double-auth friction (especially for the browser UI). Tyk adds an edge layer of rate-limiting, quota, analytics and a single public hostname. To convert to a hard gateway boundary instead, see the 'auth_token alternative' block at the bottom of this file and set use_keyless:false + auth.use_param/auth_header_name. CROSS-PROJECT CAVEAT: target_url MUST be the Opik stack PUBLIC URL — *.railway.internal only resolves inside the SAME Railway project, and this Tyk gateway is a DIFFERENT project. We target the Railway-issued public hostname (stable, TLS-terminated by Railway) rather than fabricaai.amabileai.com.br to avoid Cloudflare-proxy -> Tyk -> Cloudflare-proxy hairpinning.
name: opik-observability

_auth_token_alternative._comment: HARD GATEWAY BOUNDARY variant — replace the top-level use_keyless/auth with these and re-POST. This forces an x-tyk-authorization-style API token at the gateway BEFORE traffic reaches Opik. Use only if you want Tyk to gate access independently of Opik's own key. Browser-UI access then requires injecting the header (awkward), so this is best for SDK/machine-to-machine-only exposure of /observability/api/.
